summary: SafeUriHandlerTest is a clean, TDD-first Robolectric suite. All 11 TDD anchors from task 6 and every enumeration item in TAC-13 (2 allowed + 9 blocked) have a matching @Test with behavioural assertions that pass the litmus test: removing the scheme-whitelist or the startActivity call in SafeUriHandler causes each test to fail. Robolectric hygiene (@Config(sdk=[33]), ApplicationProvider, shadowOf, clearNextStartedActivities in @Before) is consistent with the rest of the project (ErrorLogTest, MediaUtilsTest, MultimodalContentsBuilderTest, AppSettingsRepositoryTest). No critical or high issues. A few low-severity suggestions only.

findings.issue: Two distinct inputs ('not a uri at all' and empty string) are exercised in a single @Test with only one terminal assertNull. If a future regression made SafeUriHandler silently forward the empty string but still block the non-URI (or vice-versa), the queue-based shadowOf().nextStartedActivity would return the first queued intent and the test would catch it, but the diagnostic would hide which input caused the failure. Also, the test does not call clearNextStartedActivities between the two openUri calls so it's coupling two independent cases.
findings.description: Merging two cases into one @Test reduces diagnostic granularity and weakens TDD anchor fidelity (the anchor 'malformed_blocked' is singular but covers two inputs).

findings.issue: The defensive runCatching around startActivity (ActivityNotFoundException / SecurityException path) is not exercised by any test. Under Robolectric, ACTION_VIEW with http/https does not throw even without a registered browser, so the swallow branch is dead code from the test's perspective. Not a critical gap — the branch is defensive and the happy path is covered — but an explicit negative test would document the silent-failure contract.
findings.description: If a refactor accidentally removed runCatching and introduced a crashy startActivity, tests would still pass in Robolectric.

findings.issue: Unused import: android.content.Context is imported but never referenced (handler takes an Application directly via ApplicationProvider). Minor hygiene.

Test names use snake_case Java identifiers (http_allowed, intent_blocked, malformed_blocked). Kotlin idiom for test names is backticked natural-language sentences (e.g., `http scheme opens ACTION_VIEW intent`). The current names match the TDD anchor list verbatim, which is valuable for traceability — trade-off, not a defect. Flagging only as style.
findings.description: Readability could be improved; current naming is acceptable and deliberately traceable to task anchors.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep as-is (anchor traceability > style), OR adopt backticked names and update task.md TDD Anchor section to match. Example: fun `http scheme is allowed`() { ... }. No action recommended.

findings.issue: Android's Uri.parse does not normalise the scheme case; 'HTTP://EXAMPLE.COM' yields scheme 'HTTP', which is NOT in ALLOWED_SCHEMES ({'http','https'}) and would therefore be silently blocked. Current tests only probe lowercase schemes, so this behaviour (arguably a bug: valid http link silently dropped) is not pinned.
findings.description: Neither D25 nor TAC-13 mandate a case-insensitive policy, but the whitelist as coded is case-sensitive and untested. A future 'fix' to lowercase the scheme would not break any test, and a future regression doing the reverse would equally be undetected.

tech_spec_TAC_13.blocked_malformed_and_empty: covered (line 108, both cases in single test)
tech_spec_TAC_13.test_fake_counts_startActivity: satisfied via Robolectric shadowOf(application).nextStartedActivity (effectively count==0 for blocked, count==1 for allowed — asserted by assertNull / assertNotNull)

test_pyramid_balance.assessment: healthy — aligns with tech-spec 'Unit-only pyramid' (L409); E2E-Compose is explicitly out of scope with manual smoke as substitute (AC-22).

robolectric_hygiene.runner: RobolectricTestRunner — matches project convention (ErrorLogTest, MediaUtilsTest, MultimodalContentsBuilderTest, AppSettingsRepositoryTest)
robolectric_hygiene.sdk: @Config(sdk = [33]) — consistent with all other Robolectric tests in repo
robolectric_hygiene.includeAndroidResources: app/build.gradle.kts L43 sets unitTests.isIncludeAndroidResources = true — required for Robolectric + resources
robolectric_hygiene.dependencies: junit + robolectric + androidx-test-core added to :app testImplementation (L77-79)

taskRequired.reason: Only low-severity suggestions. Task TDD anchor (11/11) and TAC-13 (2 allowed + 9 blocked) are fully covered with litmus-test-passing assertions. No critical gaps.

about_screen_unit_testability_note: AboutScreen currently does I/O (context.assets.open('about.md')) directly in the Composable via remember { ... }. Tech-spec's Unit-only pyramid explicitly scopes UI-Compose tests out (L409). The asset-read-with-fallback logic could be extracted to a pure function (readAboutContent(assets: AssetManager): String with IOException fallback) and unit-tested, but this is not required by task 6 AC or tech-spec testing strategy. Flagging for awareness only — no finding.
